content:
10 Productivity Tips for Developers As a developer, staying productive is crucial for meeting deadlines and maintaining a healthy work-life balance. Here are ten practical tips to help you boost your productivity:
1. Use Keyboard Shortcuts Mastering keyboard shortcuts for your IDE and operating system can save you hours of time. Learn the most common shortcuts for tasks you perform frequently.
2. Take Regular Breaks The Pomodoro Technique is a great way to stay focused: work for 25 minutes, then take a 5-minute break. After four cycles, take a longer break.
3. Write Clean Code Writing clean, well-documented code reduces debugging time and makes maintenance easier. Follow best practices for your programming language.
4. Use Version Control Effectively Learn advanced Git techniques like branching, stashing, and rebasing. This will help you manage code changes more efficiently.
5. Automate Repetitive Tasks Use scripts and tools to automate repetitive tasks like testing, deployment, and …
title: 10 Productivity Tips for Developers

content:
Fountain Pen
• Smooth and stable writing, no ink blots or skips
• Ergonomic grip for comfortable long-time use
• Durable nib, suitable for daily practice and homework
• Refillable ink design, economical and eco-friendly
• Simple and classic appearance, suitable for students
Carbon Gel Pen
• Extra-smooth 0.5mm fine point for clear writing
• Quick-dry ink to prevent smudging
• Strong ink concentration, ideal for exams and official writing
• Lightweight body, reduces hand fatigue during long use
• Affordable and easy to replace
Why Choose These Pens?
• Perfect for school, homework, exams, and notes
• Comfortable grip for students’ developing hands
• Reliable quality at a student-friendly price
• Suitable for daily use and long-term writing
title: Student Fountain Pen & Carbon Gel Pen

content:
• New strikes: US President Donald Trump said most of Iran’s military installations have been “knocked out” and that new strikes today targeted Iranian leadership. Israel also struck a compound belonging to a group responsible for electing Iran’s next supreme leader, an Israeli source told CNN.
• Iran death toll: At least 780 people have been killed by US and Israeli bombing across Iran, the semi-official Tasnim news agency reported, citing the Iranian Red Crescent.
• Oil markets roiled: The price of gasoline in the US just had its largest one-day increase since 2005, as vessels have effectively stopped going through the critical Strait of Hormuz. Oil continued to rise and stocks fell.
title: 我的第一篇博客
